title: carlina baetica
intro: Carlina baetica, also known as Serratula baetica, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as having antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The herbal preparations of Carlina baetica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
